mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 8.0 percent, labor market deterioration triggers immediate equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in over 60 percent of observations during expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4 percent monthly threshold forces markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of months where CPIAUCSL growth surpasses 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms genuine labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending forecasts and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows, or fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic momentum that directly supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no predictive power for 20-day forward returns, with a t-statistic below 1.5 over any 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside stagnant nonfarm payrolls signals a supply-side contraction that erodes profit margins, triggering rapid negative equity repricing during downturn regimes.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline simultaneously during recession regimes.
